what_it_does: This file is an orienteering terrain-entry ledger record for the `counterpart` workspace: it documents the project's identity as the 'Design DNA Pack', its filesystem location, git state, size, activity dates, and the four company files it references, plus verification evidence.
observations: Frontmatter marks it as a `terrain-entry` with `register: descriptive`, `status: unconfirmed`, and `relation: connected`.
It asserts that `counterpart` is the only item in `~/repos` that references `/home/tim/company`.
All four company references are prose/comment path-pointers, not code imports.
The references point to two runtime files and one build-prep contract (cited twice).
The real git repo is the inner `design/` directory, on `master` and +93 commits ahead of `origin/main`.
Working-dir root is not itself a git repo.
Size is 920M, dominated by a 413M backup tarball and ~341M git-ignored reference assets.
Only 6 of 1,582 files have been read.
The bespoke decision-card renderer in `unit-view.js` was retired on 2026-06-19 in favor of a generic archetype.
The RHM voice spec treats two company runtime files as the live corpus to extract spoken voice from.
standouts: Counterpart is the sole `~/repos` project that points back at `~/company`.
Inner git history is +93 commits ahead of origin, with recent commits labeled `DNA-REPO-FACTS:`.
It is a design/spec substrate rather than a runnable service.
The decision-card host contract is referenced both in code comments and design docs.
Voice-standard spec explicitly derives from live runtime files.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with ledger-style fields (`type`, `register`, `relation`, `coverage`, `connection_evidence`).
Quotes source lines verbatim with file paths and line numbers.
Distinguishes decimal and binary size units.
Records git nuance at both working-dir and inner-project levels.
Classifies references by kind (prose pointer vs import) and relation strength.
Maintains neutral, evidence-first tone without ranking.
concerns: Status is `unconfirmed` even though the entry itself reports all four references as verified verbatim.
Coverage is extremely low: only 6 of 1,582 files read.
Most of the 920M footprint is a backup tarball and ignored assets that are not inspected.
The `connected` relation is inferred from documentation pointers, not runtime coupling.
No explanation of what the +93 local commits contain or why they are unpushed.
notes: A densely evidenced ledger node that maps a large design/spec workspace to a handful of company runtime and build-prep files, while carefully noting its own limited file coverage.
questions: Why is status `unconfirmed` if all four company references were verified verbatim?
What do the +93 unpushed commits in `design/` contain?
What are the ~341M of git-ignored reference/source assets?
Will the inner `design/` repo be pushed to `origin/main` or remain local?
How does `unit-view.js` actually consume the decision-card host contract in practice?
What concrete DNA tokens, registries, templates, and dials exist beyond the self-description?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to record and verify `counterpart`'s place and connections in the company terrain; the file does exactly that, with extensive verbatim evidence and size/git detail.
apparent_themes: Design substrate as an addressed, typed, queryable language
Company identity layer / Design DNA Pack
Operator surface, decision-card host, and RHM voice as consuming surfaces
Live runtime files as a corpus for voice/design extraction
intention_signals: The entry is trying to anchor a sprawling design/spec workspace into the company map by proving it reads from and must plug into live runtime and build-prep contracts, while reconciling the project's grand self-description with the actual files found.
novelty: A design/spec repository that frames itself as a queryable address-registry/wayfinder/graph substrate, and that is the only `~/repos` project linked to `~/company` entirely through documentation pointers rather than code imports.
contribution: It preserves a verified ledger node for a large design/spec workspace, capturing its self-described addressing scheme, the exact company files it points to, and the distinction between prose references and runtime coupling, so a fused system can locate and reason about the Design DNA Pack without treating it as a service dependency.
summary_for_embedding: An orienteering terrain-entry for the `counterpart` design/spec workspace (Design DNA Pack) at `/home/tim/repos/counterpart`, documenting its inner git state, large tarball/ignored assets, and four verified prose references to company runtime and build-prep files.
intention_for_embedding: This file was created to map and verify `counterpart`'s connections to the company identity layer, runtime voice corpus, and decision-card host contract while flagging its limited inspection coverage.
